published_prior_art.cite: ArmstrongKolesar2018OptimalInference and KolesarRothe2018DiscreteRD give fixed-cutoff honest endpoint logic, not selected routing-threshold bounds
ArmstrongKolesar2020SimpleHonest indexes intervals by smoothness budgets but does not define a selected-cutoff sign-certification curvature threshold

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_offline_policy_joint_misspec uses active-face switching in a different offline-policy quantile-sensitivity problem, not calibrated-score endpoint geometry

published_prior_art.cite: BachocPreinerstorferSteinberger2016PostSelection, ArmstrongKolesar2018BandwidthSnooping, and ChernozhukovChetverikovDemirerDufloHansenNeweyRobins2018DML cover adjacent pieces but not generated-score same-sample selected-threshold honest inference

novelty_flags.label: related_work / comparator framing
novelty_flags.one_line: The prose names Kotte2026UCCI, Bouchard2026EscalationWorthIt, and fixed-cutoff honest-RD papers as comparators, but no statement is phrased as a strict tightening, equivalence frontier, or non-representability result against a published comparator.

soundness_flags.label: thm:selected-sharp-set
soundness_flags.one_line: The extension class H_*(P,M) omits law-realizability and range constraints implied by Delta in [-1,1], and m_P(s)=E[Delta|C=s] needs a positive-mass or version convention at support points.

soundness_flags.label: conj:postselect-honest-ci
soundness_flags.one_line: The local-linear coverage statement lacks primitive density, variance, effective-local-sample, and generated-regressor conditions, and the h_n asymptotics are not coherent with a finite discrete score-support regime.

soundness_flags.label: ass:score-estimable
soundness_flags.one_line: DML supports cross-fitting and orthogonal-score nuisance handling, but it does not by itself make L2-estimability of a generated monotone calibration score at rate r_n a standard condition.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: ass:selection-margin / conj:postselect-honest-ci
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Positive separation of the selected population threshold is assumed rather than derived.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The honest-CI conjecture is conditional on an isolated selected threshold and does not cover ties or near-ties.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: ass:score-estimable / conj:postselect-honest-ci
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The first-stage calibrated-score rate r_n is supplied as a high-level condition.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Coverage is conditional on a score-estimation regime strong enough to make r_n=o(h_n), subject to retagging or reciting the assumption correctly.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add law-realizability constraints to H_*(P,M): bounded range for the latent conditional net-gain curve, a convention for m_P at zero-mass support points, and a sharpness route showing every admissible extension is induced by some bounded outcome law.
Either add a comparator-facing statement against Kotte2026UCCI or Bouchard2026EscalationWorthIt, or downgrade the prose from comparator language to application motivation.
Retag ass:score-estimable as a high-level novel condition or cite isotonic/generated-regressor rate results directly, then state the primitive conditions under which r_n=o(h_n) is plausible.
Split the inference conjecture into continuous-score and discrete-score versions, with separate local sample-size and standard-error definitions for each regime.

recommended_next_step: Continue revise on this angle; the selected-cutoff partial-ID kernel is field-level plausible, but the extension class, inference regime, and comparator framing need repair before Stage 0.
